name: Blavod Black Vodka
description: Blavod is triple-distilled British wheat vodka colored not with additive dye but with a natural catechu extract — the same compound used in traditional inks. The result is an ink-black spirit that pours clean and finishes crisp, upending every visual expectation of what vodka should look like.

name: Hypnotiq
tagline: Cognac, vodka, and tropical fruit in electric blue.
description: A neon-blue liqueur blending French vodka, cognac, and tropical fruit juices. Hypnotiq trades subtlety for spectacle — it exists to be a visual centerpiece as much as a flavor, making it a favorite base for high-contrast layered pours.

name: Ancho Reyes
description: Made from rehydrated ancho chiles macerated for months before distillation, following a recipe dating to 1927 in Puebla. Deep smoked-pepper heat balanced with a raisin-like sweetness — built to add controlled fire to dark, spirit-forward builds.

name: Obsidian Artisanal Gin
description: A small-batch London Dry base infused with juniper, black cardamom, and a touch of smoked lapsang souchong tea, finished with activated charcoal filtration reversed to leave a deliberate ink-black tint. Built for bartenders who want a gin that changes the color of everything it touches.
